mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or correlation with PAYEMS < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, improving corporate margins and stock valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high INDPRO periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or INDPRO's predictive power drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 5% signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE < 5% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or UNRATE's predictive accuracy < 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong aggregate demand, boosting corporate revenues and stock prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction term coefficient < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) increase borrowing costs, reducing corporate profits and equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS > 4.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or predictive power < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) prompts central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in CPIAUCSL > 2% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or correlation < -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal strong demand conditions, boosting corporate revenues and stock prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-demand expansion periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction term coefficient < 0.05.
